text: For the MVP, FireGlass adopts a hybrid topology: sensors publish to an MQTT broker, the Node.js backend subscribes, and the dashboard connects via WebSocket plus a REST API rather than directly to MQTT.
Could the dashboard skip the WebSocket layer entirely and connect directly to the MQTT broker using mqtt.js for a simpler topology?
Direct browser-to-MQTT connections cannot enforce role-based permission scoping or server-side audit logging of telemetry reads at the broker level.
The Helion HelionLink v2.3 firmware and protocol documentation is unavailable, blocking sensor integration; Helion only returned an auto-reply promising a response within 5-7 business days.

John reverse-engineered the TG-400 payload (sensor_id, timestamp, temp_c, humidity_pct, signal_strength, device_battery_pct, plus a CRC-16 checksum) but the heartbeat interval, partial-packet behavior, and alarm structure remain unknown.

The team will build a TypeScript payload decoder on the reverse-engineered fields, mock the unknown fields, and validate against real payloads in the field once Helion responds or testing confirms them.
During the Milan field visit, Unit C dropped its connection every 5-10 minutes while units A and B stayed online.
Unit C's intermittent drops are probably caused by WiFi interference at Alberto's multi-floor office, though a faulty power outlet has not been ruled out.
device_battery_pct reads 100% on all three mains-powered units, which is expected for mains power but arguably the firmware should report null instead.
Offline mode works for inspection-form data but not for sensor readings; when connectivity drops, no telemetry can be logged.
The client-facing field-testing update framed Milan as 3-of-3 installed and 2-of-3 streaming live data, downplaying the Unit C drop as a local WiFi coverage issue.
The internal field notes characterized the Milan visit more bluntly, flagging Unit C drops, no offline sensor logging, and a photo-upload size-limit failure as real problems.

To recover the timeline, offline sync for sensor readings, advanced PDF reporting, and the mobile app are deferred to Phase 2 while core CRUD, real-time ingestion, RBAC, audit logs, and basic inspection forms stay in the MVP.
Internally the team described the timeline as tight, with roughly two sprints lost to Helion unknowns and Milan connectivity rework, leaving seven weeks to launch.
To the client, the same situation was framed only as a potential 1-2 week slip with the CRM and field-management modules described as on track.
Argus Safety Group ASG-FP Level 2 certification requires dual-signed PDF reports with EPFC-2201 metadata and is non-negotiable because it gates NordShield Insurance coverage for resellers' clients.
The compliance reporting module is front-loaded into Sprints 5-7 ahead of operational sensor features at Alberto's request.
In the scope-adjustment meeting CSV export was promoted to Tier 1, deferring maintenance scheduling and the notification system to post-launch because there was time for only one.
Alberto asks whether the sub-second real-time alerts he recalls proposing at kickoff are still in scope or have been descoped.

FG-001 causes duplicate sensor readings under concurrent writes (>10/sec) due to a missing unique constraint on (sensor_id, timestamp_ms) and an unsynchronized MQTT cursor; the workaround throttles polling to under 2 readings/second.

FG-002 means the system cannot distinguish an offline device from a legitimate zero-temperature reading because the RLS policy drops NULL readings and there is no heartbeat, risking missed fire events and blocking go-live.
FG-004: field technician John Smith cannot view readings from installations he commissioned because the RLS policy requires a direct user_id match while devices are assigned to the company.
The production MQTT broker choice (managed Mosquitto cloud, AWS IoT Core, or on-prem) is unresolved and depends on load-testing data not yet gathered.
Whether to rename the MQTT topic namespace from 'fire-glass/' to 'fireglass/' was raised but left unresolved because docs and the mock simulator already use the hyphenated form.
